Commit: 57653b8e42d69ec35f68a59857bad4d0f07994a3
Test: TestReadRequestBody_Uncapped_512MiB against real server package
Result: PASS — 512 MiB body read in 577 ms, handler received all 512 MiB, no cap triggered.

Trace verified at /Users/bytedance/Desktop/demo/ollama/server/cloud_proxy.go:289-301
  readRequestBody calls io.ReadAll(r.Body) unconditionally.
  Zstd branch at line 89 wraps with MaxBytesReader(20 MiB), but ONLY if
  Content-Encoding: zstd. Any plain POST skips the cap entirely.

HTTP server at routes.go:1811 sets no Handler-level body cap, no MaxHeaderBytes,
no ReadTimeout. Gin has no default BodyLimit middleware enabled.

allowedHostsMiddleware at routes.go:1608-1644 only filters when bound to
loopback — if the server bound to a non-loopback (e.g., 0.0.0.0) the middleware
short-circuits at line 1615-1618 and does not block remote hosts. This means
LAN-reachable Ollama processes (OLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0, Docker, WSL2) have no
network-level barrier between the attacker's POST and the unbounded ReadAll.

Endpoints inheriting the sink:
  /v1/chat/completions, /v1/completions, /v1/embeddings,
  /v1/responses, /v1/images/generations, /v1/images/edits,
  /v1/messages, /api/experimental/web_search, /api/experimental/web_fetch

Verdict: The vulnerability is real and reproducible. Exploitability depends on
reachability (default is loopback, but 0.0.0.0 is a documented common config).
